subject: Sociology
title: Global Health Inequality
description: Learn Global Health Inequality in one minute.

hook.text: Life expectancy in Japan is 84. In Sierra Leone, it's 54. Where you're born decides how long you live.

buildup.text: Global health inequality reflects differences in wealth, infrastructure, nutrition, and access to medical care.

discovery.text: Preventable diseases still kill millions in poor nations while wealthy nations spend billions on cosmetic procedures.

twist.text: COVID-19 exposed health inequality globally. Wealthy nations hoarded vaccines while poorer nations waited in line.

climax.text: Health inequality isn't about biology. It's about global structures that distribute resources based on wealth, not need.

punchline.text: Your zip code predicts your health more than your genes.

quiz.question: What primarily drives global health inequality?
options: Differences in wealth, infrastructure, and access to care
